action: Open a multi-bin position, choose Full exit, reduce the percentage or deselect a positive bin, then restore Full exit.

expectedAssertions.statement: Full exit selects 100% of every live positive owner bin for the exact pair and displays that complete scope.
Reducing percentage or omitting a positive bin removes the Full exit label and recalculates burns and minima.
Restoring Full exit refreshes the complete live bin set before simulation and wallet handoff.

negativePaths.trigger: A positive bin is deselected or reduced below 100%.

prerequisites: The connected owner has positive balances in at least two bins of one pair.
RPC and indexer can enumerate the exact owner and pair.
recovery: Choose Full exit again and refresh the live owner-bin enumeration.
Proceed only after the complete set is revalidated and simulated.
title: Full exit covers every live positive bin

action: Load a full-exit review at a reconciled head, then exercise complete, stale, capped, mismatched, and analytics-price-unavailable source states.

expectedAssertions.statement: Complete indexer enumeration and same-head RPC balances, reserves, supply, and simulation enable the raw exit.
Stale, capped, incomplete, or head-mismatched execution data disables submission and names the untrusted authority.
Missing analytics valuation alone leaves raw token exit amounts executable and marks valuation unavailable.

negativePaths.trigger: Indexer pagination caps or its head differs from RPC state.
USD pricing is unavailable while indexer completeness and RPC truth remain valid.

prerequisites: Indexer, RPC, and analytics states can be controlled independently.
recovery: Retry enumeration and RPC reads until their exact head reconciles.
Continue without USD valuation when execution authorities are complete.
title: Exit uses complete indexer and live RPC authority

action: Build removal plans containing fractional balances, duplicate IDs, foreign-owner IDs, wrong-pair IDs, empty IDs, and values that round to zero.

expectedAssertions.statement: Every computed burn is at most the selected live owner balance for that exact pair and bin.
Zero-burn bins are omitted only when another selected bin burns positively, and every omission is disclosed.
All-zero, duplicate, empty, wrong-owner, and wrong-pair plans are rejected before simulation or wallet handoff.

negativePaths.trigger: Percentage rounding produces zero burn for some or all selected bins.
The plan contains a duplicate, empty, foreign-owner, or wrong-pair bin ID.

prerequisites: Fixture balances include dust and non-dust bins.
Removal plans can be inspected before simulation.
recovery: Remove invalid identifiers and refresh live balances.
Increase the amount or leave the position unchanged when every burn rounds to zero.
title: Removal rounding and bin ownership fail safely

action: Exit one-sided and fully converted positions from a wrapped-native pair in native-output and wrapped-output modes.

expectedAssertions.statement: A legitimate zero minimum for one asset remains visible while the nonzero asset retains minimum protection.
Review and calldata agree on native ETH unwrap or wrapped-token output, recipient, minima, and value.
A failed native receive or unwrap remains reverted and never produces success.

negativePaths.trigger: The recipient rejects native ETH or unwrap transfer reverts.
Displayed output policy differs from the encoded router method.

prerequisites: A one-sided position exists in a wrapped-native pair.
Both output policies are available for inspection.
recovery: Select an output policy compatible with the recipient.
Refresh balances and simulate corrected calldata before retry.
title: One-sided exit preserves output policy and minima

action: Review and execute withdrawal with absent, sufficient, stale, externally revoked, wrong-pair, and wrong-operator ERC-1155 approval states; reload while pending.

expectedAssertions.statement: Approval review shows full pair and operator addresses, persistent all-bin scope, live state, and revocation instructions.
Sufficient live approval skips the request; absent, stale, or revoked approval requires fresh preflight.
Approval and removal retain separate intent, pending, receipt, replacement, reorg, and reload states.
Wrong-pair or wrong-operator approval produces no removal simulation or wallet request.

negativePaths.trigger: Approval is revoked or belongs to another pair or operator after review.
Approval or removal is rejected, reverted, replaced, orphaned, or reloaded while pending.

prerequisites: The owner has a removable position.
Approval can change externally and the journal persists across reload.
recovery: Refresh exact pair-wide approval and request it only when required.
Resume the journal entry or retry from a fresh review after terminal failure.
title: Pair-wide operator approval and removal lifecycle

action: Complete a partial removal and full exit, reload after the position row disappears, then begin a new withdrawal intent.

expectedAssertions.statement: Canonical removal reconciles wallet receipts, gas, and remaining live shares.
After full exit removes the row, hash and canonical receipt remain in History across reload.
A new withdrawal clears prior form success without deleting its History record.
Indexer or analytics lag remains downstream reconciliation and cannot alter canonical receipt state.

negativePaths.trigger: The receipt is canonical before downstream owner views update.
A new withdrawal opens after an earlier success.

prerequisites: A position supports partial and full removal.
History and journal persist across reload.
recovery: Reconcile downstream views to the receipt.
Inspect the prior transaction in History while reviewing the new intent separately.
title: Removal receipts survive position disappearance

action: Inspect whether revoke-operator, revoke-allowance, or transfer-position controls are exposed; if a control is available, execute it and refresh owner views, otherwise verify that accurate external revocation instructions appear without a placeholder control.

expectedAssertions.statement: Any exposed review names the exact asset or pair, spender or operator, owner, recipient when applicable, and persistent impact.
An exposed action simulates and submits only against fresh permission or balance state.
After a canonical receipt, Portfolio, pool position, approval state, and actions refresh consistently.
If optional actions are not implemented, no placeholder or nonfunctional control appears and accurate external revocation instructions remain available.

negativePaths.trigger: Permission or ownership changes after review of an exposed action.
The deployment lacks an in-app revoke or transfer action.

prerequisites: The owner has a permission or position whose available management path can be inspected.
recovery: Refresh live state and rebuild review for an exposed action.
Use accurate external revocation instructions when no in-app action exists.
title: Optional revoke and transfer actions disclose impact

action: Attempt full exit below, at, and above the safe batch boundary; reject a later batch, reload, transfer a new bin to the owner, and resume.

expectedAssertions.statement: Chain-derived estimation determines safe batches and discloses transaction count and non-atomic risk.
Each batch refreshes the exact-head remaining set, respects live balances, and has its own intent, hash, and receipt.
Rejection, revert, reload, reorg, or new bins resume without replaying completed burns.
Full exit completes only when every live positive bin is zero; unsupported safe batching blocks before wallet handoff.

negativePaths.trigger: A later batch fails after earlier batches become canonical.
A new bin arrives or the head advances between batches.
Estimation cannot derive a safe batch size.

prerequisites: An owner fixture exceeds the single-transaction boundary.
Per-batch simulation and journal persistence are available.
recovery: Resume from a fresh enumeration of remaining bins.
If safe batching cannot be established, leave the position unchanged and explain the blocker.
title: Gas-aware full exit batches resume safely
